MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Code Enforcement Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: P. Dias, CSR: Leticia Perez.
Ernest Llorente, Deputy City Attorney, for plaintiff City and County of S.F.
Allison S.Y. Chang, Esq., and Syma Ahmad, Esq., for Defendant: Diana E. Giusto-Graves, present in court.
Mr. Llorente reported to the Court that no progress has been made and that the code violations have not been addressed. At the time of inspection, the Inspector noticed the presence of a trailer parked, illegally, in front of the house. Photos of the subject property at 39 Waterville Street, San Francisco, Ca. were presented for the Court's review. Mr. Llorente made a motion to the Court that a Receiver be appointed to take over the management of the property. Ms. Allison S. Y. Chang, requested a 2 month continuance for a further status report. Both parties stipulated to an inspection date of February 6, 2006 at 10 A.M. of the subject property. Matter is continued to February 9, 2006 at 2:00 P.M. in Dept. 218.
Mr. Llorente to prepare an Order.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Code Enforcement Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Carl Salceda.
Ernest Llorente, Deputy City Attorney, for plaintiff City and County of S.F.
Syma Ahmad and Allison Chang, Esq. for Defendant: Diana E. Giusto-Graves, present in court with Jacqueline Guisto.
Mr. Llorente reported to the Court the inspection done at the subject property on Oct. 24, 2005, photos were submitted to the Court for review.
Mr. Llorente reported of the progess and condition of the property: garage door cover by tarp, hard wiring of lighting, damage wall, kitchen infestation. The work had stopped due to the dispute between Jacquelina and Diana Guisto and Violations were not abated and signed off by the Dept(s).
Ms. Chang reported, smoke-alarm has been installed and motion the court for a dismissal, request is denied in court.
The Court cited violations on prior violations notices and what need to be corrected and decided to continued the matter for addtional work to be completed.
Jacqueline Guisto agreed to go back to the property on a Wed. between 10 A.M. and 3 P.M., while the Guistos are out of the house, and to complete any work in progress.
Parties stipulated for an inspection date of Dec. 12, 2005 of the subject property.
Matter is continued to Dec. 15, 2005 at 2:00 P.M. in Dept. 218.
Mr. Llorente to prepare an Order.

Ernest Llorente, Deputy City Attorney, for plaintiff City and County of S.F.
Allison Chang, Esq. for Defendant: Diana E. Giusto-Graves, also present in court.
Mr. Llorente reported to the Court the inspection done at the subject property on April 11, 2005, photos were submitted to the Court for review. Defendant's brother, Mr. Larry Brusco, helped with the clean up of debris from the garage, living room and kitchen area. Photos show progress toward abatement of the public nuisance, but more clean out is necessary.
Ms. Chang also reported that the work could be completed by the end of the summer, and also moved for dismissal.
Mr. Llorente opposed the motion, unless the garage and back room are cleared.
The Court denied Defendant's motion in open court.
Parties stipulated for an inspection date of June 27, 2005 at 10 A.M. of the subject property, for progress of the cleaning and to submit photos to the Court at the next hearing.

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Code Enforcement hearing this day. Commissioner: Sue Kaplan;, Clerk: P. Dias; Reporter: Leticia Perez. Yvonne Mere, Esq. Deputy City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco. Also present, Certified Law Student, Nicholas Rosenberg. Defendant Diana E. Guisto aka Dianne Graves present in pro per. Property located at 39 Waterville Street, San Francisco. Ms. Mere reported on the progress made in hauling away a truck load of debris and that Ms. Graves has agreed to haul and additional truck load of debris from the property. MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Code Enforcement hearing this day.

Ernest Llorente, Deputy City Attorney present with Mary Collins from the Adult Protective Services and witness: Elaine Merre, were sworn and give oral testimony to the Court regarding Defendant's lack of effort to comply with the Stipulated Injuction and Order of the Court.

Osc Re: Contempt; Initiating Declaration Of The Deputy City Attorney Ernest H. Llorente
Off Calendar Nov-24-2003 Continued To Feb-06-04 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Code Enforcement hearing this day.
Commissioner Sue Kaplan Clerks: S. Ravinantapricha/V. Mack CSR: Janet Pond.
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney Ernest Lorente, Esq. for Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco.
Mary Collins, MSW, Adult Protective Services for Plaintiff.
Defendant Diana Giusto Graves in pro per, with witness Sam Sandoval.
After hearing the evidence presented, the matter arises from a 7/7/03 code enforcement inspection of the property located at 39 Waterville, San Francisco, with a follow-up inspection of 8/14/03 in which inspectors were denied access to the property.
The court schedules a re-inspection date for the City under the following timelines:
1. Defendant is to visit SSI offices on 11/24/03 of her own accord or be taken to the offices on 12/1/03 by a APS representative.
2. Defendant's friend, San Sandoval, agrees to help in removing a minimum of 3 truckloads of debris by 1/25/04.
3. The City is to inspect the property on FEB-04-2004 at 10:00 AM. No notice is required but the City Attorney agrees to send a reminder letter to defendant regarding this inspection date.

The matter is continued to FEB-06-2004 at 1:30 PM in Dept. 218. Plaintiff's counsel to prepare order.
